test_criteria.statement: ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSMap.swift exists and contains no MapboxMaps import
test_criteria.verify: test -f ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSMap.swift && grep -c 'import MapboxMaps' ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSMap.swift | grep '^0$' && echo PASS

test_criteria.statement: xcodebuild build succeeds after adding LSMap.swift stub
test_criteria.verify: cd ios && xcodebuild build -scheme LaneShadow -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16' -quiet 2>&1 | grep 'BUILD SUCCEEDED'
test_criteria.output: Validation summary provided with the task reports BUILD SUCCEEDED.

test_criteria.statement: LSMap.swift type declarations contain MapMode, CameraPosition, CameraFit, PolylineData, Annotation
test_criteria.verify: grep -c 'MapMode\|CameraPosition\|CameraFit\|PolylineData\|Annotation' ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSMap.swift | awk '{if($1>=5) print "PASS"; else print "FAIL"}'

acceptance_criteria.notes: Contract file exists, includes the required type set and both-platform public API signatures.
Fixture file contains the three required scenarios; route_results_three_alts contains exactly three polylines with best/alt1/alt2.
Stub has no Mapbox import, uses LSGlassPanel fallback only, and the supplied validation summary reports xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED.
Contract documents MBXAccessToken/Info.plist on iOS and secrets.xml/Gradle with MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN on Android.
Swift stub signature matches the shared contract for mode, camera, cameraFit, polylines, annotations, showFavorites, and onTap.

summary: APPROVED. The checkpoint adds the required shared contract, fixture scenarios, and iOS LSGlassPanel stub within the declared scope. The public contract remains SDK-agnostic, the stub avoids Mapbox imports, and the Swift signature aligns with the contract. All task criteria are satisfied; TC-5 is supported by the supplied validation summary reporting xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED.
